issues.title: Real-Time: Implement comments and @mentions system
issues.body:
## Description
Build the comments and discussion system for documents — inline comments on selected text, threaded replies, @mentions that send notifications, and the ability to resolve threads.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

### Part 1: Database Models

1. **Add the `Comment` model** to `prisma/schema.prisma`:
   ```prisma
   model Comment {
     id          String    @id @default(cuid())
     content     String
     resolved    Boolean   @default(false)
     createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
     updatedAt   DateTime  @updatedAt

     documentId  String
     document    Document  @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

     authorId    String
     author      User      @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])

     parentId    String?
     parent      Comment?  @relation("CommentThread", fields: [parentId], references: [id])
     replies     Comment[] @relation("CommentThread")

     // Position in document (for inline comments)
     selectionFrom Int?
     selectionTo   Int?
     quotedText    String?  // The text that was selected when the comment was made
   }
   ```

2. **Add the `Notification` model:**
   ```prisma
   model Notification {
     id          String   @id @default(cuid())
     type        String   // "mention", "comment", "reply"
     message     String
     read        Boolean  @default(false)
     createdAt   DateTime @default(now())

     userId      String
     user        User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

     documentId  String?
     document    Document? @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id])

     commentId   String?
   }
   ```

3. Run migration: `npx prisma migrate dev --name add-comments-notifications`

### Part 2: API Endpoints

4. **Comments CRUD:**
   - `POST /api/workspaces/[wId]/documents/[dId]/comments` — Create comment (include selectionFrom, selectionTo, quotedText for inline comments)
   - `GET /api/workspaces/[wId]/documents/[dId]/comments` — List all comments for a document
   - `POST /api/workspaces/[wId]/documents/[dId]/comments/[cId]/reply` — Reply to a comment
   - `PATCH /api/workspaces/[wId]/documents/[dId]/comments/[cId]` — Resolve/unresolve a thread
   - `DELETE /api/workspaces/[wId]/documents/[dId]/comments/[cId]` — Delete (author only)

5. **@Mentions parsing:**
   - When creating a comment, parse the content for `@username` patterns
   - Look up mentioned users in the workspace
   - Create a Notification for each mentioned user

6. **Notifications:**
   - `GET /api/notifications` — List current user's notifications
   - `PATCH /api/notifications/[id]` — Mark as read
   - `PATCH /api/notifications/read-all` — Mark all as read

### Part 3: UI Components

7. **Comment sidebar** in the document editor:
   - Panel on the right side of the editor (toggleable)
   - Shows all comments for the document
   - Grouped: Open threads at top, Resolved at bottom (collapsed)
   - Each comment shows: author avatar, name, timestamp, content, reply count

8. **Inline comment creation:**
   - User selects text in the editor → a "Comment" button appears near the selection
   - Clicking it opens a comment input with the selected text quoted
   - The comment is linked to the text position

9. **Comment highlights in the editor:**
   - Text that has comments should be highlighted (light yellow background)
   - Clicking highlighted text scrolls to the comment in the sidebar

10. **@Mention autocomplete:**
    - When typing `@` in a comment, show a dropdown of workspace members
    - Filter as user types more characters
    - Selecting a member inserts `@name` styled as a mention tag

11. **Notification bell** in the header:
    - Bell icon with unread count badge
    - Dropdown showing recent notifications
    - Clicking a notification navigates to the document and scrolls to the comment

## Testing This
- Select text → add comment → verify it appears in sidebar
- Reply to a comment → verify thread displays
- @mention a user → verify they get a notification
- Resolve a thread → verify it moves to resolved section
- Click notification → verify it navigates to the right document

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Comment model and Notification model created
- [ ] Comments can be created, listed, replied to, resolved, and deleted
- [ ] Inline comments are linked to text selections
- [ ] @mentions are parsed and create notifications
- [ ] Comment sidebar displays all threads
- [ ] Highlighted text in editor links to comments
- [ ] @mention autocomplete works in comment input
- [ ] Notification bell shows unread count
- [ ] Clicking notification navigates to document/comment
- [ ] Resolved threads are visually distinct

issues.title: UI: Build the landing page
issues.body:
## Description
Create a public landing page at the root URL (`/`) that introduces QuillSync and directs users to sign up or sign in.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Create the page** at `src/app/page.tsx` (this is the root page).

2. **Page sections:**

   **Hero Section:**
   - Large heading: "QuillSync" or a catchy tagline like "Collaborate. Document. Evolve."
   - Subheading: One-liner describing the product (e.g., "A collaborative knowledge platform for teams that think together")
   - Two CTA buttons: "Get Started" (→ /signup) and "Sign In" (→ /signin)

   **Features Section:**
   - 3-4 feature cards highlighting key capabilities:
     - "Real-time Collaboration" — Edit together with your team in real time
     - "Rich Document Editing" — Headings, tables, code blocks, images, and more
     - "Organized Workspaces" — Keep your team's knowledge structured and searchable
     - "Version History" — Never lose your work, restore any version
   - Each card: icon + title + short description

   **Footer:**
   - "Built with Next.js" or similar attribution
   - Links: GitHub repo, Sign Up, Sign In

3. **Styling:**
   - Modern, clean design with good whitespace
   - Responsive (looks great on mobile and desktop)
   - Use Tailwind CSS
   - If user is already logged in, redirect to `/dashboard` instead

4. **Navigation bar** at the top:
   - QuillSync logo/name on the left
   - "Sign In" and "Get Started" buttons on the right

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Landing page renders at `/`
- [ ] Hero section with CTA buttons
- [ ] Features section with 3-4 cards
- [ ] Navigation bar with sign in/up links
- [ ] Responsive design
- [ ] Logged-in users redirect to dashboard

issues.title: Offline: Implement offline editing with sync queue
issues.body:
## Description
Users should be able to continue editing documents when they lose their internet connection. When connectivity is restored, changes should sync back to the server automatically without data loss. This is one of the most complex features in QuillSync.

## Background
- **Offline-first:** The app should feel functional even without internet
- **Sync queue:** Changes made offline are queued and replayed when connection returns
- **Conflict resolution:** If another user edited the same document while you were offline, changes must merge without data loss (Yjs/CRDT handles this naturally)

## What To Do (Step by Step)

### Part 1: Detect online/offline status

1. **Create a connection status hook** at `src/hooks/useOnlineStatus.ts`:
   - Use `navigator.onLine` and listen for `online`/`offline` events
   - Return `{ isOnline: boolean }`
   - Show a banner at the top of the app when offline: "You are offline. Changes will be saved locally and synced when you reconnect."

### Part 2: Local storage for offline documents

2. **Set up IndexedDB storage** using a library like `idb` or Yjs's built-in `y-indexeddb`:
   ```bash
   npm install y-indexeddb
   ```

3. **Add IndexedDB persistence to the Yjs document:**
   ```typescript
   import { IndexeddbPersistence } from 'y-indexeddb'
   
   const indexeddbProvider = new IndexeddbPersistence(documentId, ydoc)
   ```
   This automatically stores the Yjs document in IndexedDB, so edits survive page refreshes and offline periods.

### Part 3: Sync queue for non-collaborative changes

4. **Create a sync queue** at `src/lib/syncQueue.ts`:
   - When offline, queue API calls (title changes, status changes, etc.) in IndexedDB
   - Each queued item stores: endpoint, method, body, timestamp
   - When online again, replay the queue in order
   - Remove items from queue after successful sync
   - Handle failures (retry with exponential backoff, max 3 retries)

### Part 4: Reconnection handling

5. **Handle reconnection gracefully:**
   - When the WebSocket reconnects, Yjs automatically merges local and remote changes (CRDT magic)
   - Process the sync queue for non-CRDT changes (title, status, etc.)
   - Show a brief "Syncing..." indicator, then "All changes synced"

### Part 5: Offline UI indicators

6. **Update the save status indicator:**
   - Online + saved: "Saved" (green)
   - Online + saving: "Saving..." (yellow)
   - Offline + cached: "Saved locally" (orange)
   - Reconnecting: "Syncing..." (blue)
   - Sync complete: "All changes synced" (green)

## Testing This
- Open a document, then turn off WiFi/disconnect
- Continue typing — changes should be stored locally
- Reconnect — changes should sync automatically
- Open the same document on another device — both changes should be merged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] App detects online/offline status
- [ ] Offline banner displays when disconnected
- [ ] Document edits work offline (stored in IndexedDB)
- [ ] Yjs IndexedDB persistence keeps document state across refreshes
- [ ] Sync queue processes pending changes on reconnection
- [ ] Failed syncs retry with backoff
- [ ] Save status indicator shows correct state
- [ ] No data loss during offline→online transition
- [ ] CRDT merges handle concurrent edits from offline users

issues.title: Real-Time: Implement collaborative editing with Yjs and WebSockets
issues.body:
## Description
This is the hardest and most critical feature in QuillSync — real-time collaborative editing. Multiple users must be able to edit the same document simultaneously without overwriting each other's changes. We will use **Yjs** (a CRDT library) with **TipTap's collaboration extension** and a **WebSocket server** for syncing.

## Background
- **CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Type):** A data structure that allows multiple users to edit simultaneously and automatically merges changes without conflicts
- **Yjs:** The most popular CRDT library for JavaScript — handles the merge logic
- **y-websocket:** A WebSocket provider for Yjs that syncs changes between clients via a server

## What To Do (Step by Step)

### Part 1: Set up the WebSocket server

1. **Install dependencies:**
   ```bash
   npm install yjs y-websocket @tiptap/extension-collaboration @tiptap/extension-collaboration-cursor y-prosemirror
   ```

2. **Create a WebSocket server** at `src/server/websocket.ts` (or a separate server file):
   - Use `y-websocket/bin/server.js` as a starting point, OR create a custom one using the `ws` library
   - The server should:
     - Accept WebSocket connections at `ws://localhost:1234`
     - Each document gets its own Yjs room (identified by `documentId`)
     - When a user connects to a room, sync the current document state
     - Broadcast changes from any user to all other users in the same room
   - Add a start script: `"ws-server": "node src/server/websocket.js"` or integrate with Next.js custom server

### Part 2: Integrate Yjs with TipTap

3. **Update the Editor component** to add collaboration:
   ```typescript
   import Collaboration from '@tiptap/extension-collaboration'
   import CollaborationCursor from '@tiptap/extension-collaboration-cursor'
   import { WebsocketProvider } from 'y-websocket'
   import * as Y from 'yjs'
   ```

4. **Create a Yjs document and WebSocket provider** for each document:
   ```typescript
   const ydoc = new Y.Doc()
   const provider = new WebsocketProvider('ws://localhost:1234', documentId, ydoc)
   ```

5. **Add the Collaboration extension** to TipTap:
   ```typescript
   Collaboration.configure({
     document: ydoc,
   })
   ```

6. **Add the CollaborationCursor extension** for cursor awareness:
   ```typescript
   CollaborationCursor.configure({
     provider,
     user: { name: currentUser.name, color: randomColor() },
   })
   ```

### Part 3: User presence indicators

7. **Show active collaborators** in the document header:
   - Display avatar circles for each connected user
   - Each user gets a consistent color
   - Show colored cursors with user name labels in the editor
   - When a user disconnects, their cursor disappears

### Part 4: Persistence

8. **Save Yjs document state to the database:**
   - When all users disconnect from a room (or periodically), persist the Yjs document state
   - On room creation (first user connects), load the existing document content and initialize the Yjs doc
   - This ensures documents survive server restarts

## Testing This
- Open the same document in two different browser tabs/windows
- Type in one tab — you should see changes appear in the other tab in near real-time
- Both cursors should be visible with different colors
- Close one tab — the cursor should disappear from the other

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WebSocket server runs and accepts connections
- [ ] Yjs CRDT is integrated with TipTap editor
- [ ] Two users can edit the same document simultaneously
- [ ] Changes sync in near real-time (< 500ms latency)
- [ ] User cursors are visible with names and colors
- [ ] Active collaborator avatars show in document header
- [ ] Document state persists to database when users disconnect
- [ ] No data loss when two users type in the same area simultaneously
- [ ] Supports 5+ simultaneous editors without degradation

issues.title: Setup: Add ESLint, Prettier, and Husky pre-commit hooks
issues.body:
## Description
Set up code quality tooling — linting, formatting, and pre-commit hooks to keep the codebase clean.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Configure ESLint** (Next.js already includes it):
   - Extend the existing `.eslintrc.json` with:
     - `plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended`
   - Install needed packages:
     ```bash
     npm install --save-dev @typescript-eslint/parser @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin
     ```

2. **Set up Prettier:**
   ```bash
   npm install --save-dev prettier eslint-config-prettier
   ```
   - Create `.prettierrc` with:
     ```json
     {
       "semi": false,
       "singleQuote": true,
       "tabWidth": 2,
       "trailingComma": "es5"
     }
     ```
   - Create `.prettierignore` with: `node_modules`, `.next`, `prisma/migrations`
   - Add `"prettier"` to the end of ESLint's `extends` array to disable conflicting rules

3. **Set up Husky + lint-staged:**
   ```bash
   npm install --save-dev husky lint-staged
   npx husky init
   ```
   - Configure `lint-staged` in `package.json`:
     ```json
     "lint-staged": {
       "*.{ts,tsx}": ["eslint --fix", "prettier --write"],
       "*.{json,md,css}": ["prettier --write"]
     }
     ```
   - Update `.husky/pre-commit` to run: `npx lint-staged`

4. **Add npm scripts** to `package.json`:
   ```json
   "lint": "next lint",
   "format": "prettier --write .",
   "format:check": "prettier --check ."
   ```

5. **Verify** by making a commit — the pre-commit hook should auto-format staged files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ESLint is configured for TypeScript
- [ ] Prettier is configured and integrated with ESLint
- [ ] Husky pre-commit hook runs lint-staged
- [ ] `npm run lint` works
- [ ] `npm run format` works
- [ ] Pre-commit hook auto-formats on commit

issues.title: API: Version history save and retrieval endpoints
issues.body:
## Description
Build API endpoints for saving document version snapshots and retrieving version history.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

### 1. Auto-save Versions — Update the Document PATCH endpoint
- Modify `PATCH /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents/[documentId]`
- Before updating the document, save the CURRENT state as a new `DocumentVersion`
- Only save a version if content has actually changed (compare old vs new content)
- Rate limit version creation: max 1 version per 5 minutes per document (don't create a version on every single keystroke save)

### 2. List Versions — `GET /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents/[documentId]/versions`
- **Behavior:**
  - User must be a member of the workspace
  - Return all versions for the document, ordered by `createdAt` descending (newest first)
  - Include: version id, title, author name, createdAt
  - Do NOT include full content in the list (too heavy) — only return content for a single version
  - Paginate: 20 per page, support `?page=1` query param

### 3. Get Single Version — `GET /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents/[documentId]/versions/[versionId]`
- **Behavior:**
  - Return the full version including content
  - Return `404` if version doesn't exist

### 4. Restore Version — `POST /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents/[documentId]/versions/[versionId]/restore`
- **Behavior:**
  - Save the current document state as a new version (so restoration is reversible)
  - Replace the document's title and content with the version's data
  - User must be OWNER, ADMIN, or EDITOR
  - Return `200` with the restored document

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Versions auto-created when document content changes
- [ ] Version list endpoint returns paginated results
- [ ] Single version endpoint returns full content
- [ ] Restore endpoint works and is reversible
- [ ] Rate limiting prevents excessive version creation

issues.title: Database: Create Version History model
issues.body:
## Description
Create the database model for storing document version history snapshots.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Add the `DocumentVersion` model** to `prisma/schema.prisma`:

   ```prisma
   model DocumentVersion {
     id          String   @id @default(cuid())
     title       String
     content     Json?
     createdAt   DateTime @default(now())

     documentId  String
     document    Document @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

     authorId    String
     author      User     @relation("VersionAuthor", fields: [authorId], references: [id])
   }
   ```

2. **Update the `Document` model** — add:
   ```prisma
   versions  DocumentVersion[]
   ```

3. **Update the `User` model** — add:
   ```prisma
   documentVersions  DocumentVersion[]  @relation("VersionAuthor")
   ```

4. **Run the migration:**
   ```bash
   npx prisma migrate dev --name add-document-version-model
   ```

## Key Design Decisions
- `onDelete: Cascade` — versions are deleted when the parent document is deleted
- Each version stores a complete snapshot (title + content) for easy restoration
- `authorId` tracks who made the change that created this version

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DocumentVersion model is defined
- [ ] Relations to Document and User work
- [ ] Cascade delete is configured
- [ ] Migration runs successfully

issues.title: UI: Build search bar with results dropdown
issues.body:
## Description
Add a search bar to the header that lets users search for documents across all their workspaces, with a live results dropdown.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Add a search input** to the Header component:
   - Search icon on the left
   - Placeholder: "Search documents..."
   - Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl/Cmd+K focuses the search bar

2. **Search behavior:**
   - Start searching after the user types 2+ characters
   - Debounce input by 300ms to avoid excessive API calls
   - Call `GET /api/search?q={query}`
   - Show a loading spinner while fetching

3. **Results dropdown:**
   - Appears below the search input as an overlay
   - Each result shows: document title (bold), workspace name (subtitle), author name
   - Clicking a result navigates to that document's editor page
   - Show "No results found" if search returns empty
   - Show max 10 results with "View all results" link at bottom

4. **Keyboard navigation:**
   - Arrow Up/Down to navigate results
   - Enter to open the selected result
   - Escape to close the dropdown

5. **Close behavior:**
   - Dropdown closes when clicking outside
   - Dropdown closes when pressing Escape
   - Dropdown closes after navigating to a result

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search bar appears in the header
- [ ] Ctrl/Cmd+K focuses the search input
- [ ] Results dropdown shows matching documents
- [ ] Debounced search works (no excessive API calls)
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Clicking a result navigates to the document
- [ ] Empty state shows "No results found"

issues.title: Search: Build full-text search API endpoint
issues.body:
## Description
Build a search API that lets users find documents across their workspaces by searching titles, content, tags, and author names.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Create the API route** at `src/app/api/search/route.ts`.

2. **Implement `GET /api/search?q=searchterm`**:
   - **Query params:**
     - `q` (required) — the search query string
     - `workspaceId` (optional) — filter to a specific workspace
   - **Behavior:**
     - Only search documents the user has access to (user must be a member of the document's workspace)
     - Search across: document title, document content (extract text from JSON), author name
     - Return results sorted by relevance (title matches first, then content matches)
     - Limit to 20 results
     - Return: document id, title, workspace name, author name, snippet (matching text context), updatedAt

3. **For content search**, extract plain text from the TipTap JSON content. Create a helper function `extractTextFromContent(json: any): string` that recursively walks the JSON and extracts all text nodes.

4. **For now, use Prisma's `contains` filter** (case-insensitive) for basic search:
   ```typescript
   where: {
     OR: [
       { title: { contains: query, mode: 'insensitive' } },
       { author: { name: { contains: query, mode: 'insensitive' } } }
     ]
   }
   ```
   Note: Full-text search with PostgreSQL `tsvector` can be added later as an optimization.

5. **Response format:**
   ```json
   {
     "results": [
       {
         "id": "doc_123",
         "title": "API Design Guide",
         "workspaceName": "Engineering",
         "authorName": "Jane Doe",
         "snippet": "...the REST API should follow...",
         "updatedAt": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
       }
     ],
     "total": 1
   }
   ```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search endpoint works with query string
- [ ] Only returns documents user has access to
- [ ] Searches across title and author name
- [ ] Results include document metadata and snippet
- [ ] Optional workspace filter works
- [ ] Returns 400 if query is empty

issues.title: UI: Build Workspace Settings page
issues.body:
## Description
Create a settings page for workspaces where owners and admins can manage workspace details and members.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Create the page** at `src/app/(main)/workspace/[workspaceId]/settings/page.tsx`.
   - Only accessible to OWNER and ADMIN roles
   - Redirect others to the workspace page with an error toast

2. **General Settings section:**

- [ ] Member removal works with confirmation
- [ ] Invite member form works

issues.title: UI: Build Document Editor page with auto-save
issues.body:
## Description
Create the document editor page that loads a document, renders the TipTap editor, and auto-saves changes.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Create the page** at `src/app/(main)/workspace/[workspaceId]/document/[documentId]/page.tsx`.

2. **Page structure:**
   - **Title input** at the top — large text input (no border, just text) for the document title

   - When the title changes, debounce 500ms, then PATCH the document
   - When the editor content changes (via onChange), debounce 500ms, then PATCH the document
   - Show a save indicator: "Saving..." → "Saved" (with timestamp)

5. **Read-only mode:**
   - If user is a VIEWER, render the editor in read-only mode (no toolbar, no editing)
   - Show a banner: "You have view-only access to this document"

6. **Document status:**
   - Show the current status (Draft, Review, Published, Archived) as a badge
   - Allow OWNER/ADMIN/EDITOR to change status via a dropdown

## Acceptance Criteria

issues.title: API: Document CRUD endpoints
issues.body:
## Description
Build the API endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting documents within a workspace.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

### 1. Create Document — `POST /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents`
- **Request body:** `{ title?: string, parentId?: string }`
- **Behavior:**
  - User must be OWNER, ADMIN, or EDITOR in the workspace
  - Create a new Document with the user as author
  - Default title: "Untitled"
  - Default content: `null` (empty document)
  - If `parentId` is provided, set the parent relationship (nested page)
  - Return `201` with the created document

### 2. List Documents — `GET /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents`
- **Behavior:**
  - User must be a member of the workspace (any role)
  - Return all top-level documents (where `parentId` is null)
  - Include child count for each document
  - Support query param `?parentId=xxx` to list children of a specific document
  - Return `200` with array of documents

### 3. Get Document — `GET /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents/[documentId]`
- **Behavior:**
  - User must be a member of the workspace
  - Return the full document including content, author info, and children
  - Return `404` if document doesn't exist

### 4. Update Document — `PATCH /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents/[documentId]`
- **Request body:** `{ title?: string, content?: JSON, status?: DocumentStatus }`
- **Behavior:**
  - User must be OWNER, ADMIN, or EDITOR
  - Update only the provided fields
  - Return `200` with updated document

### 5. Delete Document — `DELETE /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/documents/[documentId]`
- **Behavior:**
  - User must be OWNER or ADMIN, or the document's author
  - Delete the document and all its children (cascade)
  - Return `200` with success message

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 endpoints work correctly
- [ ] Workspace membership checked on every endpoint
- [ ] Role-based write permissions enforced
- [ ] Nested document (parent-child) support works
- [ ] Proper HTTP status codes and error messages

issues.title: API: Workspace member management endpoints
issues.body:
## Description
Build API endpoints for managing workspace members — inviting, removing, and updating roles.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

### 1. Invite Member — `POST /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/members`
- **Request body:** `{ email: string, role: "ADMIN" | "EDITOR" | "VIEWER" }`
- **Behavior:**
  - Only OWNER or ADMIN can invite members
  - Look up the user by email
  - If user doesn't exist, return `404` with "User not found"
  - If user is already a member, return `409` with "User is already a member"
  - Create a WorkspaceMember entry with the specified role
  - Return `201` with the new member info

### 2. List Members — `GET /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/members`
- **Behavior:**
  - User must be a member of the workspace to view
  - Return all members with their name, email, role, and joinedAt
  - Return `200` with array of members

### 3. Update Member Role — `PATCH /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/members/[memberId]`
- **Request body:** `{ role: "ADMIN" | "EDITOR" | "VIEWER" }`
- **Behavior:**
  - Only OWNER can change roles
  - Cannot change the OWNER's own role via this endpoint
  - Return `200` with updated member

### 4. Remove Member — `DELETE /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/members/[memberId]`
- **Behavior:**
  - OWNER can remove anyone (except themselves)
  - ADMIN can remove EDITOR and VIEWER
  - Members can remove themselves (leave workspace)
  - OWNER cannot leave (must transfer ownership first)
  - Return `200` with success message

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 endpoints work correctly
- [ ] Role-based permission checks enforced
- [ ] Edge cases handled (duplicate invite, owner leave, etc.)
- [ ] Proper HTTP status codes used

## Description
Build the API endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting workspaces.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

- **Request body:** `{ name: string, description?: string }`
- **Behavior:**
  - Get the authenticated user from the session
  - Create a new Workspace with the user as the owner
  - Also create a WorkspaceMember entry with role `OWNER` for the creating user
  - Return `201` with the created workspace

### 2. List User's Workspaces — `GET /api/workspaces`
- **Behavior:**
  - Get the authenticated user from the session
  - Fetch all workspaces where the user is a member (via WorkspaceMember)
  - Include the member count for each workspace
  - Return `200` with the array of workspaces

### 3. Get Single Workspace — `GET /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]`
- Create route at `src/app/api/workspaces/[workspaceId]/route.ts`
- **Behavior:**
  - Verify user is a member of the workspace
  - Return workspace details including members list
  - Return `403` if user is not a member
  - Return `404` if workspace doesn't exist

### 4. Update Workspace — `PATCH /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]`
- **Request body:** `{ name?: string, description?: string }`
- **Behavior:**
  - Verify user is OWNER or ADMIN of the workspace
  - Update the specified fields
  - Return `200` with updated workspace
  - Return `403` if user doesn't have permission

### 5. Delete Workspace — `DELETE /api/workspaces/[workspaceId]`
- **Behavior:**
  - Verify user is OWNER
  - Delete the workspace and all related data (members, documents)
  - Return `200` with success message
  - Return `403` if user is not the owner

issues.title: Auth: Build user registration API endpoint
issues.body:
## Description
Create an API endpoint that allows new users to register with email, name, and password.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Create the API route** at `src/app/api/auth/register/route.ts`.

2. **Implement the POST handler** that:
   - Accepts a JSON body with `email`, `name`, and `password`
   - Validates all fields are present and `email` is a valid email format
   - Checks if a user with that email already exists in the database
   - If user exists, returns `409 Conflict` with message `"User already exists"`
   - Hashes the password using `bcryptjs.hash(password, 10)` (10 salt rounds)
   - Creates the user in the database using Prisma
   - Returns `201 Created` with the user object (WITHOUT the password field)

3. **Example request:**
   ```json
   POST /api/auth/register
   {
     "email": "user@example.com",
     "name": "John Doe",
     "password": "securepassword123"
   }
   ```

4. **Example success response:**
   ```json
   {
     "user": {
       "id": "clxyz...",
       "email": "user@example.com",
       "name": "John Doe"
     }
   }
   ```

5. **Example error responses:**
   - Missing fields: `400 Bad Request`
   - Email taken: `409 Conflict`
   - Server error: `500 Internal Server Error`

## Security Notes
- NEVER store passwords in plaintext — always hash with bcryptjs
- NEVER return the password hash in the response
- Validate email format on the server side

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST `/api/auth/register` creates a new user
- [ ] Password is hashed before storing
- [ ] Duplicate email returns 409
- [ ] Missing fields return 400
- [ ] Response never includes the password

issues.title: Database: Create Document model
issues.body:
## Description
Define the `Document` model in the Prisma schema. Documents are the core content unit in QuillSync — each document belongs to a workspace and has an author.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Add the `Document` model** to `prisma/schema.prisma`:

   ```prisma
   enum DocumentStatus {
     DRAFT
     REVIEW
     PUBLISHED
     ARCHIVED
   }

   model Document {
     id          String         @id @default(cuid())
     title       String         @default("Untitled")
     content     Json?          // Stores rich text as JSON (e.g., TipTap/ProseMirror JSON)
     status      DocumentStatus @default(DRAFT)
     createdAt   DateTime       @default(now())
     updatedAt   DateTime       @updatedAt

     authorId    String
     author      User           @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])

     workspaceId String
     workspace   Workspace      @relation(fields: [workspaceId], references: [id])

     parentId    String?
     parent      Document?      @relation("DocumentHierarchy", fields: [parentId], references: [id])
     children    Document[]     @relation("DocumentHierarchy")
   }
   ```

2. **Update the `User` model** — add:
   ```prisma
   documents  Document[]
   ```

3. **Update the `Workspace` model** — add:
   ```prisma
   documents  Document[]
   ```

4. **Run the migration:**
   ```bash
   npx prisma migrate dev --name add-document-model
   ```

## Key Design Decisions
- `content` is `Json?` to store rich-text editor output (TipTap/ProseMirror format)
- `parentId` self-relation enables nested pages (parent-child document hierarchy)
- `DocumentStatus` enum maps to the PRD's status indicators: Draft, Review, Published, Archived

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document model is defined with all fields
- [ ] Self-referencing relation for parent-child hierarchy works
- [ ] DocumentStatus enum is created
- [ ] Reverse relations added to User and Workspace
- [ ] Migration runs successfully

issues.title: Database: Create Workspace and WorkspaceMember models
issues.body:
## Description
Define the `Workspace` and `WorkspaceMember` models in the Prisma schema to support workspace creation and membership.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Add the `Workspace` model** to `prisma/schema.prisma`:

   ```prisma
   model Workspace {
     id          String   @id @default(cuid())
     name        String
     description String?
     createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
     updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt

     ownerId     String
     owner       User     @relation("WorkspaceOwner", fields: [ownerId], references: [id])
     members     WorkspaceMember[]
   }
   ```

2. **Add the `WorkspaceMember` model** (join table between User and Workspace):

   ```prisma
   enum WorkspaceRole {
     OWNER
     ADMIN
     EDITOR
     VIEWER
   }

   model WorkspaceMember {
     id          String        @id @default(cuid())
     role        WorkspaceRole @default(VIEWER)
     joinedAt    DateTime      @default(now())

     userId      String
     user        User          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

     workspaceId String
     workspace   Workspace     @relation(fields: [workspaceId], references: [id])

     @@unique([userId, workspaceId])
   }
   ```

3. **Update the `User` model** to add the reverse relations:
   ```prisma
   // Add these fields to the existing User model:
   ownedWorkspaces  Workspace[]       @relation("WorkspaceOwner")
   memberships      WorkspaceMember[]
   ```

4. **Run the migration:**
   ```bash
   npx prisma migrate dev --name add-workspace-models
   ```

## Key Design Decisions
- `@@unique([userId, workspaceId])` prevents a user from being added to the same workspace twice
- `WorkspaceRole` enum defines the four permission levels from the PRD: Owner, Admin, Editor, Viewer
- Workspace has a direct `owner` relation for quick ownership lookups

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Workspace model is defined with all fields
- [ ] WorkspaceMember model is defined with role enum
- [ ] User model has reverse relations added
- [ ] Unique constraint exists on (userId, workspaceId)
- [ ] Migration runs successfully

issues.title: Database: Create User model in Prisma schema
issues.body:
## Description
Define the `User` model in the Prisma schema. This is the foundational model that all other features (workspaces, documents, collaboration) depend on.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Open `prisma/schema.prisma`** and add the following `User` model:

   ```prisma
   model User {
     id          String   @id @default(cuid())
     email       String   @unique
     name        String?
     avatarUrl   String?
     password    String
     createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
     updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
   }
   ```

2. **Field explanations:**
   - `id` — Unique identifier using CUID (collision-resistant unique ID)
   - `email` — User's email, must be unique across all users
   - `name` — Optional display name
   - `avatarUrl` — Optional profile picture URL
   - `password` — Hashed password (will store bcrypt hash, NOT plaintext)
   - `createdAt` — Auto-set when user is created
   - `updatedAt` — Auto-updated when user record changes

3. **Run the migration:**
   ```bash
   npx prisma migrate dev --name add-user-model
   ```

4. **Verify** the migration was created in `prisma/migrations/` folder.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User model is defined in `schema.prisma`
- [ ] Migration file is generated and applied
- [ ] `npx prisma generate` succeeds
- [ ] All fields match the specification above

issues.title: Setup: Install and configure Prisma ORM with MongoDB
issues.body:
## Description
Set up Prisma ORM as the database layer for QuillSync, connected to a MongoDB database.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Install Prisma** as a dev dependency and the Prisma Client:
   ```bash
   npm install prisma --save-dev
   npm install @prisma/client
   ```

2. **Initialize Prisma**:
   ```bash
   npx prisma init
   ```
   This creates a `prisma/` folder with a `schema.prisma` file and a `.env` file.

3. **Configure the `.env` file** with a MongoDB connection string:

     ```Update the .env file with a MongoDB connection string:```      

      ```
       DATABASE_URL="mongodb://localhost:27017/quillsync"
      ```


       
      ```Add .env to .gitignore if not already present.```

4. **Configure Prisma Datasource**

    ```Update prisma/schema.prisma:```
    ```
      generator client {
         provider = "prisma-client-js"
       }

      datasource db {
           provider = "mongodb"
            url    = env("DATABASE_URL")
      }
     ```

5. **Create a `.env.example`** file with a placeholder connection string (no real credentials).

6. **Set up a reusable Prisma Client instance** in `src/lib/prisma.ts`:
   ```typescript
   import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client'

   const globalForPrisma = globalThis as unknown as { prisma: PrismaClient }

   export const prisma = globalForPrisma.prisma || new PrismaClient()

   if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') globalForPrisma.prisma = prisma
   ```
   This prevents creating multiple Prisma Client instances during development hot reloads.

7. **Verify** by running `npx prisma db push` (after creating a local PostgreSQL database) and confirming no errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prisma is installed and initialized
- [ ] `schema.prisma` exists with PostgreSQL as the datasource
- [ ] `.env.example` file exists with placeholder DB URL
- [ ] `.env` is in `.gitignore`
- [ ] Reusable Prisma client is created in `src/lib/prisma.ts`
- [ ] `npx prisma generate` runs without errors

issues.title: Project Setup: Initialize Next.js project with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
issues.body:
## Description
Set up the foundational project structure for QuillSync using Next.js (App Router), TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS.

## What To Do (Step by Step)

1. **Initialize a new Next.js project** using `npx create-next-app@latest` with the following options:
   - TypeScript: Yes
   - Tailwind CSS: Yes
   - ESLint: Yes
   - App Router: Yes
   - `src/` directory: Yes

2. **Create the following folder structure** inside `src/`:
   ```
   src/
   ├── app/           # Next.js App Router pages
   ├── components/    # Reusable UI components
   │   ├── ui/        # Base UI primitives (buttons, inputs, etc.)
   │   └── layout/    # Layout components (header, sidebar, etc.)
   ├── lib/           # Utility functions and shared logic
   ├── hooks/         # Custom React hooks
   ├── types/         # TypeScript type definitions
   └── styles/        # Global styles
   ```

3. **Configure Tailwind CSS** with a sensible color palette and font settings in `tailwind.config.ts`.

4. **Add a basic `README.md`** with:
   - Project name (QuillSync)
   - One-line description
   - How to install dependencies (`npm install`)
   - How to run the dev server (`npm run dev`)

5. **Verify** the dev server runs without errors at `http://localhost:3000`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Next.js project initializes and runs without errors
- [ ] TypeScript is configured and working
- [ ] Tailwind CSS is configured and working
- [ ] Folder structure is created as specified
- [ ] README.md exists with setup instructions
